he.text: The Supreme Lord said Know a genuine sannyāsī and yogī as one who without expectation of the fruits of his actions performs his duties as prescribed by the scriptures One is not a sannyāsī merely by renouncing the performance of the fire sacrifice and other prescribed duties and one is not a yogī merely by becoming physically inactive

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said Know a genuine sannyāsī and yogī as one who without expectation of the fruits of his actions performs his duties as prescribed by the scriptures One is not a sannyāsī merely by renouncing the performance of the fire sacrifice and other prescribed duties and one is not a yogī merely by becoming physically inactive

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said Know a genuine sannyāsī and yogī as one who without expectation of the fruits of his actions performs his duties as prescribed by the scriptures One is not a sannyāsī merely by renouncing the performance of the fire sacrifice and other prescribed duties and one is not a yogī merely by becoming physically inactive

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said Know a genuine sannyāsī and yogī as one who without expectation of the fruits of his actions performs his duties as prescribed by the scriptures One is not a sannyāsī merely by renouncing the performance of the fire sacrifice and other prescribed duties and one is not a yogī merely by becoming physically inactive

he.text: O Arjuna that which is known as sannyās complete renunciation you should know to be yoga since without renouncing fruitive desire no one can be a yogī

ar.text: O Arjuna that which is known as sannyās complete renunciation you should know to be yoga since without renouncing fruitive desire no one can be a yogī

tr.text: O Arjuna that which is known as sannyās complete renunciation you should know to be yoga since without renouncing fruitive desire no one can be a yogī

sw.text: O Arjuna that which is known as sannyās complete renunciation you should know to be yoga since without renouncing fruitive desire no one can be a yogī

he.text: For the yoga practitioner desirous of attaining steady yoga מדיטציה action is said to be the means When he has achieved yoga מדיטציה renunciation of all action is said to be the means of remaining in מדיטציה

ar.text: For the yoga practitioner desirous of attaining steady yoga التأمل action is said to be the means When he has achieved yoga التأمل renunciation of all action is said to be the means of remaining in التأمل

tr.text: For the yoga practitioner desirous of attaining steady yoga meditasyon action is said to be the means When he has achieved yoga meditasyon renunciation of all action is said to be the means of remaining in meditasyon

sw.text: For the yoga practitioner desirous of attaining steady yoga kutafakari action is said to be the means When he has achieved yoga kutafakari renunciation of all action is said to be the means of remaining in kutafakari

he.text: When the yoga practitioner does not become attached to the objects of the senses and to any action in pursuit of their enjoyment and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment then he is said to have attained to yoga

ar.text: When the yoga practitioner does not become attached to the objects of the senses and to any action in pursuit of their enjoyment and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment then he is said to have attained to yoga

tr.text: When the yoga practitioner does not become attached to the objects of the senses and to any action in pursuit of their enjoyment and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment then he is said to have attained to yoga

sw.text: When the yoga practitioner does not become attached to the objects of the senses and to any action in pursuit of their enjoyment and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment then he is said to have attained to yoga

he.text: One must elevate himself by the mind not degrade himself by the mind for the mind is sometimes the friend of the living being and in another situation that very mind is his enemy

ar.text: One must elevate himself by the mind not degrade himself by the mind for the mind is sometimes the friend of the living being and in another situation that very mind is his enemy

tr.text: One must elevate himself by the mind not degrade himself by the mind for the mind is sometimes the friend of the living being and in another situation that very mind is his enemy

sw.text: One must elevate himself by the mind not degrade himself by the mind for the mind is sometimes the friend of the living being and in another situation that very mind is his enemy

he.text: For one who has conquered the mind his mind is his friend For one unable to control the mind his mind works against him like an enemy

ar.text: For one who has conquered the mind his mind is his friend For one unable to control the mind his mind works against him like an enemy

tr.text: For one who has conquered the mind his mind is his friend For one unable to control the mind his mind works against him like an enemy

sw.text: For one who has conquered the mind his mind is his friend For one unable to control the mind his mind works against him like an enemy

he.text: The yogī who has conquered the mind remains deeply absorbed in yogic trance peaceful and undisturbed by cold and heat אושר and unhappiness or honour and dishonour

ar.text: The yogī who has conquered the mind remains deeply absorbed in yogic trance peaceful and undisturbed by cold and heat السعادة and unhappiness or honour and dishonour

tr.text: The yogī who has conquered the mind remains deeply absorbed in yogic trance peaceful and undisturbed by cold and heat mutluluk and unhappiness or honour and dishonour

sw.text: The yogī who has conquered the mind remains deeply absorbed in yogic trance peaceful and undisturbed by cold and heat furaha and unhappiness or honour and dishonour

he.text: One who is always satisfied within by both ידע and realisation who is always fixed in his spiritual nature who is sense-controlled and who has the vision of equality for a clod of earth a rock or gold—it is said that such a person has attained yoga

ar.text: One who is always satisfied within by both المعرفة and realisation who is always fixed in his spiritual nature who is sense-controlled and who has the vision of equality for a clod of earth a rock or gold—it is said that such a person has attained yoga

tr.text: One who is always satisfied within by both bilgi and realisation who is always fixed in his spiritual nature who is sense-controlled and who has the vision of equality for a clod of earth a rock or gold—it is said that such a person has attained yoga

sw.text: One who is always satisfied within by both ujuzi and realisation who is always fixed in his spiritual nature who is sense-controlled and who has the vision of equality for a clod of earth a rock or gold—it is said that such a person has attained yoga

he.text: Such an eminent yogī has the vision of equality towards a well-wisher a friend an enemy an indifferent person a mediator a hateful person a relative a saint and a sinner

ar.text: Such an eminent yogī has the vision of equality towards a well-wisher a friend an enemy an indifferent person a mediator a hateful person a relative a saint and a sinner

tr.text: Such an eminent yogī has the vision of equality towards a well-wisher a friend an enemy an indifferent person a mediator a hateful person a relative a saint and a sinner

sw.text: Such an eminent yogī has the vision of equality towards a well-wisher a friend an enemy an indifferent person a mediator a hateful person a relative a saint and a sinner

he.text: The yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by remaining alone in a solitary place his mind and body controlled free from desire and acquisition

ar.text: The yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by remaining alone in a solitary place his mind and body controlled free from desire and acquisition

tr.text: The yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by remaining alone in a solitary place his mind and body controlled free from desire and acquisition

sw.text: The yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by remaining alone in a solitary place his mind and body controlled free from desire and acquisition

he.text: In this way constantly absorbing his mind in מדיטציה dhyāna-yoga the yogī who has ceased hankering for material enjoyment achieves the שלום of liberation from mundanity the שלום which abides in Me in my Brahma effulgence

ar.text: In this way constantly absorbing his mind in التأمل dhyāna-yoga the yogī who has ceased hankering for material enjoyment achieves the السلام of liberation from mundanity the السلام which abides in Me in my Brahma effulgence

tr.text: In this way constantly absorbing his mind in meditasyon dhyāna-yoga the yogī who has ceased hankering for material enjoyment achieves the huzur of liberation from mundanity the huzur which abides in Me in my Brahma effulgence

sw.text: In this way constantly absorbing his mind in kutafakari dhyāna-yoga the yogī who has ceased hankering for material enjoyment achieves the amani of liberation from mundanity the amani which abides in Me in my Brahma effulgence

he.text: O Arjuna yoga cannot be practised by one who overeats undereats oversleeps or undersleeps

ar.text: O Arjuna yoga cannot be practised by one who overeats undereats oversleeps or undersleeps

tr.text: O Arjuna yoga cannot be practised by one who overeats undereats oversleeps or undersleeps

sw.text: O Arjuna yoga cannot be practised by one who overeats undereats oversleeps or undersleeps

he.text: For a person who eats relaxes and works in a regulated way and who keeps regular hours in proper measure his practice of yoga dispells all his suffering

ar.text: For a person who eats relaxes and works in a regulated way and who keeps regular hours in proper measure his practice of yoga dispells all his suffering

tr.text: For a person who eats relaxes and works in a regulated way and who keeps regular hours in proper measure his practice of yoga dispells all his suffering

sw.text: For a person who eats relaxes and works in a regulated way and who keeps regular hours in proper measure his practice of yoga dispells all his suffering

he.text: When a yogī withdraws the mind from mundane thoughts and centres it unwaveringly in the self then free from mundane desires he is said to be actually situated in yoga or linked with the Absolute

ar.text: When a yogī withdraws the mind from mundane thoughts and centres it unwaveringly in the self then free from mundane desires he is said to be actually situated in yoga or linked with the Absolute

tr.text: When a yogī withdraws the mind from mundane thoughts and centres it unwaveringly in the self then free from mundane desires he is said to be actually situated in yoga or linked with the Absolute

sw.text: When a yogī withdraws the mind from mundane thoughts and centres it unwaveringly in the self then free from mundane desires he is said to be actually situated in yoga or linked with the Absolute

he.text: It is said as a lamp-flame situated in a windless place does not waver similarly the mind of the yogī absorbed in the self never flickers in its concentration

ar.text: It is said as a lamp-flame situated in a windless place does not waver similarly the mind of the yogī absorbed in the self never flickers in its concentration

tr.text: It is said as a lamp-flame situated in a windless place does not waver similarly the mind of the yogī absorbed in the self never flickers in its concentration

sw.text: It is said as a lamp-flame situated in a windless place does not waver similarly the mind of the yogī absorbed in the self never flickers in its concentration

he.text: The mind controlled by yoga practice is tranquil pacified by detachment from mundane association The yogī remains satisfied in the Lord alone directly seeing the Supersoul through his purified consciousness

ar.text: The mind controlled by yoga practice is tranquil pacified by detachment from mundane association The yogī remains satisfied in the Lord alone directly seeing the Supersoul through his purified consciousness

tr.text: The mind controlled by yoga practice is tranquil pacified by detachment from mundane association The yogī remains satisfied in the Lord alone directly seeing the Supersoul through his purified consciousness

sw.text: The mind controlled by yoga practice is tranquil pacified by detachment from mundane association The yogī remains satisfied in the Lord alone directly seeing the Supersoul through his purified consciousness

he.text: In his spiritual enlightenment he tastes transcendental eternal bliss He never deviates from the reality

ar.text: In his spiritual enlightenment he tastes transcendental eternal bliss He never deviates from the reality

tr.text: In his spiritual enlightenment he tastes transcendental eternal bliss He never deviates from the reality

sw.text: In his spiritual enlightenment he tastes transcendental eternal bliss He never deviates from the reality

he.text: He considers that there is no greater gain in this world He is undisturbed by even the worst suffering

ar.text: He considers that there is no greater gain in this world He is undisturbed by even the worst suffering

tr.text: He considers that there is no greater gain in this world He is undisturbed by even the worst suffering

sw.text: He considers that there is no greater gain in this world He is undisturbed by even the worst suffering

he.text: Know that the attainment of such a state in which all association with unhappiness is severed is called yoga Yoga must be practised with perseverance and a heart that is never discouraged

ar.text: Know that the attainment of such a state in which all association with unhappiness is severed is called yoga Yoga must be practised with perseverance and a heart that is never discouraged

tr.text: Know that the attainment of such a state in which all association with unhappiness is severed is called yoga Yoga must be practised with perseverance and a heart that is never discouraged

sw.text: Know that the attainment of such a state in which all association with unhappiness is severed is called yoga Yoga must be practised with perseverance and a heart that is never discouraged

he.text: To practice yoga one must abandon all thoughts that give rise to desires and using the mind withdraw the senses from all material objects

ar.text: To practice yoga one must abandon all thoughts that give rise to desires and using the mind withdraw the senses from all material objects

tr.text: To practice yoga one must abandon all thoughts that give rise to desires and using the mind withdraw the senses from all material objects

sw.text: To practice yoga one must abandon all thoughts that give rise to desires and using the mind withdraw the senses from all material objects

he.text: By steadying the intelligence one should gradually still the mind and focus it within the self alone

ar.text: By steadying the intelligence one should gradually still the mind and focus it within the self alone

tr.text: By steadying the intelligence one should gradually still the mind and focus it within the self alone

sw.text: By steadying the intelligence one should gradually still the mind and focus it within the self alone

he.text: The wavering and unsteady mind should be brought back from whatever mundane objects it pursues and be held within the self alone

ar.text: The wavering and unsteady mind should be brought back from whatever mundane objects it pursues and be held within the self alone

tr.text: The wavering and unsteady mind should be brought back from whatever mundane objects it pursues and be held within the self alone

sw.text: The wavering and unsteady mind should be brought back from whatever mundane objects it pursues and be held within the self alone

he.text: The transcendental joy of self-realisation comes to the yogī whose passions are quieted who is peacefulhearted free from the defects of attachment fear and anger and always situated in the spiritual nature

ar.text: The transcendental joy of self-realisation comes to the yogī whose passions are quieted who is peacefulhearted free from the defects of attachment fear and anger and always situated in the spiritual nature

tr.text: The transcendental joy of self-realisation comes to the yogī whose passions are quieted who is peacefulhearted free from the defects of attachment fear and anger and always situated in the spiritual nature

sw.text: The transcendental joy of self-realisation comes to the yogī whose passions are quieted who is peacefulhearted free from the defects of attachment fear and anger and always situated in the spiritual nature

he.text: Thus absorbed in self-realisation the clean-hearted yogī easily attains the boundless ecstasy of contacting the divine He realises the Supersoul Such yoga is of a devotional nature

ar.text: Thus absorbed in self-realisation the clean-hearted yogī easily attains the boundless ecstasy of contacting the divine He realises the Supersoul Such yoga is of a devotional nature

tr.text: Thus absorbed in self-realisation the clean-hearted yogī easily attains the boundless ecstasy of contacting the divine He realises the Supersoul Such yoga is of a devotional nature

sw.text: Thus absorbed in self-realisation the clean-hearted yogī easily attains the boundless ecstasy of contacting the divine He realises the Supersoul Such yoga is of a devotional nature

he.text: His consciousness united with the Infinite the master of yoga sees the universal spirituality He sees the Supreme Soul within all beings and all beings within the Supreme

ar.text: His consciousness united with the Infinite the master of yoga sees the universal spirituality He sees the Supreme Soul within all beings and all beings within the Supreme

tr.text: His consciousness united with the Infinite the master of yoga sees the universal spirituality He sees the Supreme Soul within all beings and all beings within the Supreme

sw.text: His consciousness united with the Infinite the master of yoga sees the universal spirituality He sees the Supreme Soul within all beings and all beings within the Supreme

he.text: For one who sees Me in everything and everything in Me I am never lost to him and he is never lost to Me

ar.text: For one who sees Me in everything and everything in Me I am never lost to him and he is never lost to Me

tr.text: For one who sees Me in everything and everything in Me I am never lost to him and he is never lost to Me

sw.text: For one who sees Me in everything and everything in Me I am never lost to him and he is never lost to Me

he.text: The yogī who takes shelter of Me and adores Me in My non-different form as the Supersoul situated within the heart of every living being abides in Me in all circumstances

ar.text: The yogī who takes shelter of Me and adores Me in My non-different form as the Supersoul situated within the heart of every living being abides in Me in all circumstances

tr.text: The yogī who takes shelter of Me and adores Me in My non-different form as the Supersoul situated within the heart of every living being abides in Me in all circumstances

sw.text: The yogī who takes shelter of Me and adores Me in My non-different form as the Supersoul situated within the heart of every living being abides in Me in all circumstances

he.text: I consider the best of all yogīs to be the one who knows the אושר and unhappiness of all beings as his own

ar.text: I consider the best of all yogīs to be the one who knows the السعادة and unhappiness of all beings as his own

tr.text: I consider the best of all yogīs to be the one who knows the mutluluk and unhappiness of all beings as his own

sw.text: I consider the best of all yogīs to be the one who knows the furaha and unhappiness of all beings as his own

he.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan because of the fickle nature of the mind I cannot conceive how one can maintain the equilibrium in yoga that You have described

ar.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan because of the fickle nature of the mind I cannot conceive how one can maintain the equilibrium in yoga that You have described

tr.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan because of the fickle nature of the mind I cannot conceive how one can maintain the equilibrium in yoga that You have described

sw.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan because of the fickle nature of the mind I cannot conceive how one can maintain the equilibrium in yoga that You have described

he.text: O Kṛṣṇa the mind is fickle turbulent obstinate and very powerful I consider controlling it to be more difficult than controlling the wind

ar.text: O Kṛṣṇa the mind is fickle turbulent obstinate and very powerful I consider controlling it to be more difficult than controlling the wind

tr.text: O Kṛṣṇa the mind is fickle turbulent obstinate and very powerful I consider controlling it to be more difficult than controlling the wind

sw.text: O Kṛṣṇa the mind is fickle turbulent obstinate and very powerful I consider controlling it to be more difficult than controlling the wind

he.text: The Supreme Lord said O heroic Arjuna undoubtedly the mind is fickle and extremely difficult to control However O son of Kuntī it is possible by practice and detachment

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said O heroic Arjuna undoubtedly the mind is fickle and extremely difficult to control However O son of Kuntī it is possible by practice and detachment

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said O heroic Arjuna undoubtedly the mind is fickle and extremely difficult to control However O son of Kuntī it is possible by practice and detachment

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said O heroic Arjuna undoubtedly the mind is fickle and extremely difficult to control However O son of Kuntī it is possible by practice and detachment

he.text: I consider that yoga is difficult to attain for the person of unrestrained mind But one who strives to restrain the mind by the appropriate practice can succeed in yoga

ar.text: I consider that yoga is difficult to attain for the person of unrestrained mind But one who strives to restrain the mind by the appropriate practice can succeed in yoga

tr.text: I consider that yoga is difficult to attain for the person of unrestrained mind But one who strives to restrain the mind by the appropriate practice can succeed in yoga

sw.text: I consider that yoga is difficult to attain for the person of unrestrained mind But one who strives to restrain the mind by the appropriate practice can succeed in yoga

he.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa what is the destination of a person who due to his faith in the scriptures engages in yoga practice but lacking perseverence proper practice and abnegation is distracted by mundane inclinations and fails to succeed in yoga practice

ar.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa what is the destination of a person who due to his faith in the scriptures engages in yoga practice but lacking perseverence proper practice and abnegation is distracted by mundane inclinations and fails to succeed in yoga practice

tr.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa what is the destination of a person who due to his faith in the scriptures engages in yoga practice but lacking perseverence proper practice and abnegation is distracted by mundane inclinations and fails to succeed in yoga practice

sw.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa what is the destination of a person who due to his faith in the scriptures engages in yoga practice but lacking perseverence proper practice and abnegation is distracted by mundane inclinations and fails to succeed in yoga practice

he.text: O mighty hero O Kṛṣṇa having deviated from the yoga of attaining the Absolute shelterless and fallen from the paths of both action and מדיטציה is not such a person utterly lost like a scattered cloud

ar.text: O mighty hero O Kṛṣṇa having deviated from the yoga of attaining the Absolute shelterless and fallen from the paths of both action and التأمل is not such a person utterly lost like a scattered cloud

tr.text: O mighty hero O Kṛṣṇa having deviated from the yoga of attaining the Absolute shelterless and fallen from the paths of both action and meditasyon is not such a person utterly lost like a scattered cloud

sw.text: O mighty hero O Kṛṣṇa having deviated from the yoga of attaining the Absolute shelterless and fallen from the paths of both action and kutafakari is not such a person utterly lost like a scattered cloud

he.text: O Kṛṣṇa No one but You can dispel this doubt of mine Please mercifully slash it at the root

ar.text: O Kṛṣṇa No one but You can dispel this doubt of mine Please mercifully slash it at the root

tr.text: O Kṛṣṇa No one but You can dispel this doubt of mine Please mercifully slash it at the root

sw.text: O Kṛṣṇa No one but You can dispel this doubt of mine Please mercifully slash it at the root

he.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna son of Kuntī there is no loss for the unsuccessful yoga practitioner as he is not denied the אושר of the heavenly plane in this world nor is he denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul in the transcendental realm This is so O dear one because a person who performs virtuous actions never suffers an ill fate

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna son of Kuntī there is no loss for the unsuccessful yoga practitioner as he is not denied the السعادة of the heavenly plane in this world nor is he denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul in the transcendental realm This is so O dear one because a person who performs virtuous actions never suffers an ill fate

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna son of Kuntī there is no loss for the unsuccessful yoga practitioner as he is not denied the mutluluk of the heavenly plane in this world nor is he denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul in the transcendental realm This is so O dear one because a person who performs virtuous actions never suffers an ill fate

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna son of Kuntī there is no loss for the unsuccessful yoga practitioner as he is not denied the furaha of the heavenly plane in this world nor is he denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul in the transcendental realm This is so O dear one because a person who performs virtuous actions never suffers an ill fate

he.text: After residing for many years in all those heavenly planes that are attainable by the performers of great sacrifices such as the Aśvamedha the unsuccessful yoga practitioner takes birth in the home of honourable prosperous persons given to pure and honest practices

ar.text: After residing for many years in all those heavenly planes that are attainable by the performers of great sacrifices such as the Aśvamedha the unsuccessful yoga practitioner takes birth in the home of honourable prosperous persons given to pure and honest practices

tr.text: After residing for many years in all those heavenly planes that are attainable by the performers of great sacrifices such as the Aśvamedha the unsuccessful yoga practitioner takes birth in the home of honourable prosperous persons given to pure and honest practices

sw.text: After residing for many years in all those heavenly planes that are attainable by the performers of great sacrifices such as the Aśvamedha the unsuccessful yoga practitioner takes birth in the home of honourable prosperous persons given to pure and honest practices

he.text: Or he takes birth in the home or family of teachers of yoga who are dedicated to yoga practice Such a birth is more rarely attained in this world

ar.text: Or he takes birth in the home or family of teachers of yoga who are dedicated to yoga practice Such a birth is more rarely attained in this world

tr.text: Or he takes birth in the home or family of teachers of yoga who are dedicated to yoga practice Such a birth is more rarely attained in this world

sw.text: Or he takes birth in the home or family of teachers of yoga who are dedicated to yoga practice Such a birth is more rarely attained in this world

he.text: O son of Kuru within one of these situations the חוכמה he had attained in his previous life is revived and he again strives for success

ar.text: O son of Kuru within one of these situations the الحكمة he had attained in his previous life is revived and he again strives for success

tr.text: O son of Kuru within one of these situations the bilgelik he had attained in his previous life is revived and he again strives for success

sw.text: O son of Kuru within one of these situations the busara he had attained in his previous life is revived and he again strives for success

he.text: Despite himself he is carried by the yoga practice of his previous life In his search he surpasses the fruitive rituals of the Vedas

ar.text: Despite himself he is carried by the yoga practice of his previous life In his search he surpasses the fruitive rituals of the Vedas

tr.text: Despite himself he is carried by the yoga practice of his previous life In his search he surpasses the fruitive rituals of the Vedas

sw.text: Despite himself he is carried by the yoga practice of his previous life In his search he surpasses the fruitive rituals of the Vedas

he.text: Striving harder than before the yogī’s consciousness is cleansed of all worldly attachment and he achieves the fruit of many lifetimes of yoga practice—he attains the supreme destination

ar.text: Striving harder than before the yogī’s consciousness is cleansed of all worldly attachment and he achieves the fruit of many lifetimes of yoga practice—he attains the supreme destination

tr.text: Striving harder than before the yogī’s consciousness is cleansed of all worldly attachment and he achieves the fruit of many lifetimes of yoga practice—he attains the supreme destination

sw.text: Striving harder than before the yogī’s consciousness is cleansed of all worldly attachment and he achieves the fruit of many lifetimes of yoga practice—he attains the supreme destination

he.text: The yogī is superior to persons engaged in severe austerities such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow superior to the person of ידע who worships Brahma and superior to the person of action Know this to be My conclusion Therefore O Arjuna be a yogī

ar.text: The yogī is superior to persons engaged in severe austerities such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow superior to the person of المعرفة who worships Brahma and superior to the person of action Know this to be My conclusion Therefore O Arjuna be a yogī

tr.text: The yogī is superior to persons engaged in severe austerities such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow superior to the person of bilgi who worships Brahma and superior to the person of action Know this to be My conclusion Therefore O Arjuna be a yogī

sw.text: The yogī is superior to persons engaged in severe austerities such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow superior to the person of ujuzi who worships Brahma and superior to the person of action Know this to be My conclusion Therefore O Arjuna be a yogī

he.text: The best of all yogīs is the devotee who has full faith in the authoritative pure devotional scriptures and who adores Me with all his heart hearing and singing My divine glories rendering all services unto Me This is My opinion

ar.text: The best of all yogīs is the devotee who has full faith in the authoritative pure devotional scriptures and who adores Me with all his heart hearing and singing My divine glories rendering all services unto Me This is My opinion
